conference.name: 5th Bank of Italy, Bocconi University, EIEF and CEPR Conference on Financial Stability and Regulation

extras.venue: Carlo Azeglio Ciampi Centre for Monetary and Financial Education, Banca d'Italia
extras.organizer: Banca d'Italia, Bocconi University – Baffi, EIEF, CEPR
extras.description: The conference brings together leading world scholars and policy-makers to discuss financial stability and regulation.

extras.keynote_speaker: Tano Santos (Columbia Business School and CEPR)
extras.opening_remarks: Chiara Scotti, Board Member, Banca d'Italia
program_committee: Marco Pagano (University of Naples Federico II, EIEF, CEPR)

scrape_metadata.notes: Program extracted from PDF. Sessions structured from PDF content.

sessions.session_title: Session I - Monetary Policy Transmission through Bank Balance Sheets

papers.title: Securities Losses and the Bank Collateral Channel of Monetary Transmission
Quantitative Tightening: The Bank Liquidity-Duration Nexus

sessions.session_title: Session II - Information, Platforms, and Algorithms in Credit Markets

papers.title: Black Box Credit Scoring and Data Sharing

sessions.session_title: Session III - Safe Asset Market Fragility, Dealers, and Central Bank Interventions

papers.title: Central Bank Balance Sheet and Treasury Market Disruptions
Dealers Information and Liquidity Crises in Safe Assets

sessions.session_title: Session IV - Distressed Firms, Credit Misallocation, and Macroeconomic Trade-offs

papers.title: Extend-and-Pretend in the U.S. CRE Market

sessions.session_title: Session V - Bank Regulation, Supervision, and Credit Allocation

papers.title: Mitigating the risks of deregulation: The role of supervisory attention

sessions.session_title: Session VI - Non-banks and Their Interconnections with Banks

papers.title: The Value of Contingent Liquidity from Banks to Nonbank Lenders
Life Insurers' Private Credit Investments and Annuity Market Share Capture
